title: Delicate Turkish Vine Leaf Dolma
description: A winey, aerated, light Turkish dish featuring dehydrate and vegetable shortening, wrapped in vine leaves for a refreshing flavor.
instructions: Blanch the vine leaves in boiling water for 1 minute, then drain and pat dry.
Rinse the rice until water runs clear, then heat a pan with 1 tablespoon of vegetable shortening and sauté the onion and garlic for 3 minutes.
Add the rice, crushed tomato puree, dill, lemon juice, salt, and pepper to the pan and cook for 5 minutes.
Transfer the hot rice mixture to a bowl and let it cool slightly.
Place a spoonful of the rice mixture onto the center of each vine leaf, fold the sides, and roll tightly.
Arrange the rolled dolmas in a pot and pour enough water to cover them, then add a splash of wine for aroma.
Bring the water to a gentle boil, reduce heat, and simmer for 25 minutes.
Serve the dolmas chilled or at room temperature, garnished with a lemon wedge.
